cont_clean: De 3 typiske fertilitetsbehandlingsformer er IVF, ICSI og IUI. IVF står for "In Vitro Fertilisering", også kendt under daglig tale som "reagensglasbefrugtning". IVF dækker over to typer: 1) den konventionelle, klassiske IVF, hvor sædceller og ægceller placeres i en petriskål. 2) ICSI, forkortet fra "Intra Cytoplasmatisk Sædcelle Injektion", eller også kendt som mikroinsemination, indebærer at én sædcelle sprøjtes med en tynd nål direkte ind i ægcellen. Således er denne type IVF altså endnu mere assisteret end den konventionelle. IUI (Intrauterine Insemination) er klassisk insemination, og foregår dermed ikke "in vitro" men derimod "in vivo". IVF, ICSI og IUI henviser til selve måden hvorpå befrugtningen sker. Ved IVF/ICSI er det (befrugtningen) en mindre del af et større billede. Først bliver kvindens æggestokke og æg stimuleret med hormoner, så der modnes flere æg end naturligt. Derefter følger en ægudtagning, hvor forhåbningen er at få flere æg ud på én gang, så man har flere forsøg til at få befrugtningen til at lykkes. Herefter følger der en protokol for forberedelsen af både æg og sædceller, som involverer rensning samt udvælgelse af celler med bedst kvalitet. Befrugtningen sker enten ved konventionel IVF eller ICSI, hvorefter udviklingen af det befrugtede æg - nu kaldet embryo - følges tæt. Når (eller hvis) embryonet har udviklet sig til blastocyst-stadiet, på ca. 5. dagen, bliver embryoet 'transfereret' ind i kvinden. Dette kaldes også ægoplægning eller embryo transfer, hvor man håber på implantation - altså at ægget sætter sig fast i livmoderen. Dette bliver typisk først bekræftet ved en efterfølgende graviditetstest. #ivftreatment #invitrofertilization #icsi #assistedreproductivetechnology #fertilitytreatment

cont_clean: This new Commentary by Fatih Aktoz, Nikolaos P Polyzos, and Christophe Blockeel explains what lies "inside the box" of regression analysis in IVF research. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rbmo.2026.105800 In IVF studies, where outcomes are influenced by many biological and clinical factors, regression models offer a structured way to address complexity. However, an adjusted result is not automatically more reliable than a crude comparison. The meaning of a regression estimate depends on the outcome chosen, the variables included, the assumptions made, and the structure of the data. Without understanding these elements, even a statistically impressive model may mislead. In the same way that a single p value cannot capture the full meaning of a study, a single regression model cannot define clinical reality. A regression model is a structured summary of data built on specific assumptions. For this reason, it should not be seen as the final answer, but as one more step toward understanding. Detective Walley received cell phone images from another officer investigating a separate case. She believed the original phone search was conducted with a warrant and used the images to obtain an arrest warrant. The plaintiff later sued, claiming the original search was warrantless and that Walley violated his Fourth Amendment rights by reviewing the evidence. The Fifth Circuit granted qualified immunity, ruling that simply reviewing images obtained by another officer does not constitute a new search. cont_clean: 🚔 What Is Qualified Immunity? Many people have heard the term 'qualified immunity', but few understand what it actually means. Qualified immunity is a legal doctrine that can protect government officials, including police officers, from personal liability in certain civil rights lawsuits. In many cases, an officer may avoid being held personally responsible unless it can be shown that they violated a "clearly establishe" constitutional right. ⚖️ But qualified immunity is not absolute. Officers and government officials can still be held accountable when the facts and law support a civil rights claim. Every situation is unique, and the details matter. cont_clean: Most people expect burnout to look like complete exhaustion. But burnout often introduces itself much earlier. It can look like re-reading the same email three times because your focus is gone. Forgetting simple tasks you never used to miss. Feeling unusually irritated by small problems. Dreading Monday before the weekend is even over. Quietly losing interest in work you once enjoyed. These signs are easy to dismiss as stress, being busy, or just having a bad week.
